contract.goal: Ship plugins/jelly-loom/cli/lib/manifest.ts — a hand-rolled TOML serializer + parser for jelly's constrained, write-once project manifest shape, plus the JellyManifest type. Isolated as its own unit because a hand-rolled parser is the highest-risk piece of Phase 1.3. No verb wires it yet (U4 jelly plan is the first consumer).
acceptance_criteria: plugins/jelly-loom/cli/lib/manifest.ts exports JellyManifest, JellyPhase, JellyConfig types; stringifyManifest(m): string; parseManifest(raw): JellyManifest.

The hand-rolled TOML supports exactly the subset the manifest needs: top-level key=value (string/integer/boolean), a [config] table, [[phases]] array-of-tables, arrays-of-strings (depends_on). Comments (#) and blank lines tolerated on parse.

Unsupported TOML constructs are rejected, not silently mis-parsed — multiline strings, dotted keys, nested tables, floats, datetimes, inline tables throw manifest-invalid-toml naming the offending line.
Validation: parseManifest throws manifest-invalid-toml (syntax), manifest-schema-invalid (missing required keys), manifest-unsupported-version (schema_version !== 1).
Round-trip property: parseManifest(stringifyManifest(m)) deep-equals m for any valid manifest.
Tests cover round-trip, each value type, [[phases]] array, depends_on (empty + populated), each error code, comment/blank tolerance, rejection of each unsupported construct.
npm test passes (rises by the manifest test count).
rules_applied: PLAN.md § Phase 1.3 Exit (TOML manifest: identity + write-once [config] + write-once [[phases]] + references) + § Decisions log (TOML for PR-diff readability + comments; no per-PR mutation) + § Exit requirements (manifest sees zero writes after creation in steady state).
The abstraction domain mode jelly-guild shipped: support the bounded subset, reject the rest — no speculative generality.
Hand-roll posture (operator decision); zero-dep substrate (package.json untouched).
plugins/loom/cli/lib/manifest.ts + errors.ts (reference for the manifest-lib + structured-error shape, NOT for the mutable fields).
Panel: dropped the auto-derived evaluator-react-api (documented mis-derivation for Node .ts — see memory derive-panel-react-api-misderivation). Ran contract-fit + test-unit + naming.
disqualifiers: Wiring the lib into any verb (U4 jelly plan is the first consumer).
A general-purpose TOML library dependency (hand-roll posture; zero-dep).
Supporting TOML constructs the manifest doesn't use (no speculative generality — reject what we don't need rather than half-implement).
Per-PR-mutable fields in the manifest type (current_branch, latest_checkin, per-phase status/branch) — those are loom's shape; jelly's manifest is write-once.
Editing the CLI shell (jelly.ts) or other plugins.
inputs: projects/2026-05-25-jelly/PLAN.md § Phase 1.3 + § Decisions log + § Exit requirements.
plugins/jelly-loom/cli/jelly.ts (the CliContext/DispatchResult types + the plugin the lib lives in).

actions: Wrote plugins/jelly-loom/cli/lib/errors.ts: JellyError class (code + message + candidates + toPayload()) mirroring loom's LoomError. Supporting infra the manifest lib throws through; jelly-loom declares its own (not imports loom's) per the parallel-plugin posture.

Wrote plugins/jelly-loom/cli/lib/manifest.ts (~330 lines): JellyManifest/JellyPhase/JellyConfig types (write-once — no current_branch/latest_checkin/per-phase status/branch); stringifyManifest (canonical-order emission with TOML basic-string escaping for backslash/quote/control-chars); parseManifest (the hand-rolled reader). The reader: string-aware comment stripping (a # inside a basic string is not a comment), a value parser supporting basic strings / integers / booleans / string-arrays, and explicit rejection (manifest-invalid-toml with line numbers) of multiline strings, dotted keys, non-[config]/[[phases]] table headers, floats, datetimes, inline tables, non-string array elements, unterminated strings, no-equals lines, and content-after-a-closed-string. Validation throws manifest-schema-invalid (missing/wrong-type keys) and manifest-unsupported-version (schema_version != 1, checked first).

Wrote plugins/jelly-loom/cli/lib/manifest.test.ts (27 tests, 6 describe blocks): stringifyManifest (canonical order, trailing newline, non-integer-version writer guard); round-trip deep-equal across 5 shapes (sample / zero-phases / single-phase / quotes-and-backslashes / multi-element depends_on); value types; comment + blank-line tolerance incl the #-inside-a-string case; schema validation errors (each missing-key path + unsupported-version); rejection of 10 unsupported constructs each asserting the specific manifest-invalid-toml code.

Verified: npm test → 1033 passed across 70 files (was 1006; +27 from manifest.test.ts). Panel (contract-fit + test-unit + naming): all three approved on first run. Dropped evaluator-react-api (documented mis-derivation; Node .ts, no React).

changes_since_previous: Second unit of Phase 1.3; composes on U1's shell. Ships the hand-rolled TOML manifest lib — the highest-risk piece of the phase, isolated for focused review. The lib is the foundation U4 (jelly plan) needs to WRITE manifest.toml at project birth. Not yet wired into any verb. Carries lagged pr-opened event for PR #59 in events.jsonl.
notes_for_pr: Highest-risk unit of Phase 1.3 — a hand-rolled TOML parser. The safety contract is explicit rejection over silent mis-parsing: every construct outside the supported subset (string/integer/boolean scalars, [config] table, [[phases]] array-of-tables, string arrays) throws manifest-invalid-toml with the offending line number. 10 rejection tests assert the specific code one-to-one with the unsupported-construct list. The round-trip property (parse∘stringify deep-equals) is the other half of the safety net, tested across 5 shapes including adversarial quotes/backslashes.

Zero-dep posture held: no TOML library; package.json untouched. The substrate's 9 plugins still carry zero runtime dependencies. The hand-rolled subset is deliberately minimal (the abstraction domain mode's no-speculative-generality rule) — float/datetime/inline-table support are rejection paths, not half-built features.

Write-once shape: JellyManifest has NO mutable fields (no current_branch, latest_checkin, per-phase status/branch — those are loom's per-PR-mutated shape). jelly's manifest is written once by jelly plan (U4) and untouched in steady state (PLAN.md § Exit requirements: 'manifest sees zero writes after creation'). The `status` field is documented as archival-only, not per-PR.

Advisory from contract-fit (non-gating): the round-trip property is total only for escapeString-producible values (backslash/quote/\n/\t/\r). A value carrying some other raw control char would round-trip lossily — but the manifest's fields (titles, slugs, ISO dates, paths, phase numbers) can't carry such characters, so within the contract's domain the round-trip is total. Worth a note if a future manifest field takes free-form prose.

Advisory from test-unit (non-gating): the boolean-support test asserts indirectly — it parses `flagged = true` as an extra key in a [[phases]] block and asserts the surrounding phase still parses, rather than directly asserting the parser produced the boolean true. Defensible because the manifest schema has no boolean field (so there's no direct round-trip surface) and parseValue isn't exported. A future tightening: export parseValue for a focused value-level unit test, or add a real boolean field if the schema ever needs one.

errors.ts (JellyError) is supporting infrastructure introduced alongside the manifest lib (the first lib code that throws structured errors). It mirrors loom's LoomError. All future jelly-loom verbs (U3-U6) will throw through it.
Substrate-state carry-over: lagged pr-opened event for PR #59 in events.jsonl rides with this commit.

U3 (jelly research) is next — the first verb, and the first consumer of the CliContext/DispatchResult types from the shell (though research doesn't touch the manifest; U4 jelly plan is the manifest lib's first consumer).
